intended_use: SugarBug is a radiological, automated, concurrent read, computer-assisted detection software intended to aid in the detection and segmentation of caries on bitewing radiographs. The device provides ad
intended_use_full: SugarBug is a radiological, automated, concurrent read, computer-assisted detection software intended to aid in the detection and segmentation of caries on bitewing radiographs. The device provides additional information for the dentist to use in their diagnosis of a tooth surface suspected of being carious. Sugarbug is intended to be used on patients 18 years and older. The device is not intended as a replacement for a complete dentist's review or their clinical judgment that takes into account other relevant information from the image, patient history, and actual in vivo clinical assessment.
ground_truth: Consensus labels of 3 US licensed general dentists with an average of 27 years of clinical experience
other_metrics: Lesion-level improvement in sensitivity: 0.134 (0.066, 0.206); Mean FPPI aided 0.325 (0.128, 0.310) vs unaided 0.328 (0.102, 0.331); DICE coefficient (lesion annotation similarity) aided 0.740 (0.733, 0.747) vs unaided 0.695 (0.688, 0.702); Standalone model lesion-level sensitivity 0.686 (0.655, 0.717), mean FPPI 0.231 (0.111, 0.303), DICE coefficient vs ground truth 0.746 (0.724, 0.768)
